title: nf-core/msproteomics pipeline parameters
description: Mass spectrometry proteomics preprocessing (DIA, DDA LFQ, TMT)

input_output_options.title: Input/output options
input_output_options.description: Define where the pipeline should find input data and save output data.

input.description: Path to comma-separated samplesheet CSV with columns: sample, spectra (optional: condition, label, fraction).
input.help_text: Provide a simple CSV samplesheet with per-sample data. Experiment-level parameters (enzyme, modifications, mass tolerances) are set in nextflow.config. See [usage docs](https://nf-co.re/msproteomics/usage#input) for format details.

organism.description: Organism name for SDRF generation (e.g., Homo sapiens, Mus musculus, Saccharomyces cerevisiae).

dissociation_method.description: Fragmentation/dissociation method used for MS/MS acquisition.

outdir.description: The output directory where the results will be saved. You have to use absolute paths to storage on Cloud infrastructure.

email.help_text: Set this parameter to your e-mail address to get a summary e-mail with details of the run sent to you when the workflow exits. If set in your user config file (`~/.nextflow/config`) then you don't need to specify this on the command line for every run.

multiqc_title.description: MultiQC report title. Printed as page header, used for filename if not otherwise specified.

config_file.description: Path to a custom Nextflow config file to override pipeline defaults.
config_file.help_text: When provided, this config file is loaded instead of the mode-specific config. For advanced use only -- most users should use the --mode parameter and per-parameter overrides instead.

acquisition_method.description: Data acquisition method for SDRF generation.

labelling_type.description: Labelling type for SDRF generation (e.g., label free sample, TMT).

is_phospho_data.description: Flag indicating phosphoproteomics data (adds enrichment metadata to SDRF).

workflow_mode_options.title: Workflow mode
workflow_mode_options.description: Define the analysis mode for the pipeline.

mode.description: Analysis mode: 'diann' for DIA-NN workflows, 'fragpipe' for FragPipe-based DDA/TMT workflows.
mode.help_text: Controls which analysis engine the pipeline uses. Use 'diann' for DIA (Data-Independent Acquisition) analysis with DIA-NN. Use 'fragpipe' for DDA LFQ, TMT label check, or generic FragPipe workflows.

tmt_mode.help_text: Only used when --mode fragpipe. Controls the TMT analysis workflow: 'labelcheck' for labeling efficiency QC, 'quant' for TMT quantification. When not set, the pipeline runs a DDA LFQ workflow by default.

database_options.title: Protein database options
database_options.description: Protein database FASTA file and related options.

database.description: Path to a protein database FASTA file.
database.help_text: A protein FASTA file for database search. For DIA workflows, if not provided, the pipeline uses a reference proteome based on the organism parameter (human, mouse, or yeast). For FragPipe-based workflows (DDA LFQ, TMT), the pipeline auto-resolves the database from the organism parameter.

diann_speclib.description: Pre-built DIA-NN spectral library (.speclib) to skip in-silico library generation.
diann_speclib.help_text: If a spectral library has already been generated by DIA-NN, provide it here along with the FASTA to skip the library generation step.

philosopher_database.description: Path to a pre-built Philosopher database FASTA (with decoys and contaminants).
philosopher_database.help_text: A FASTA file already processed by Philosopher (containing decoy sequences and contaminants). If provided, the pipeline skips the PHILOSOPHER_DATABASE step. If not provided, the pipeline auto-resolves the database from the organism parameter.

diann_empirical_assembly_lib.description: Pre-built DIA-NN empirical library to skip library assembly step.

diann_empirical_assembly_log.description: Log file from a previous empirical library assembly (provides mass accuracy settings).

dia_search_options.title: DIA-NN search options
dia_search_options.description: Parameters for DIA-NN in-silico library generation and database search. These apply to DIA workflows only (DIA, DIAPhos). Leave blank to use pipeline defaults optimized for your workflow and instrument.

enzyme.help_text: Enzyme name used by DIA-NN for in-silico digestion. The pipeline translates the enzyme name to the appropriate DIA-NN --cut rule automatically.

fixed_mods.description: Fixed modifications in Unimod name format, e.g. 'Carbamidomethyl (C)'.
fixed_mods.help_text: DIA-NN --fixed-mod flag. Leave empty for no fixed modifications. Format: 'Name (Site)' using Unimod modification names. See https://www.unimod.org/modifications_list.php for valid names.

variable_mods.description: Variable modifications in Unimod name format, e.g. 'Oxidation (M)'.
variable_mods.help_text: DIA-NN --var-mod flag. Default is Oxidation of Methionine. Format: 'Name (Site)' using Unimod modification names. See https://www.unimod.org/modifications_list.php for valid names. Common examples: 'Oxidation (M)', 'Acetyl (Protein N-term)', 'Phospho (STY)'.

max_mods.description: Maximum number of variable modifications per peptide.

allowed_missed_cleavages.description: Maximum number of missed enzyme cleavages.

min_peptide_length.description: Minimum peptide length for in-silico digestion.

max_peptide_length.description: Maximum peptide length for in-silico digestion.

diann_library_mass_acc.description: Fragment ion mass accuracy (ppm) for in-silico spectral library generation.

diann_library_ms1_acc.description: MS1 mass accuracy (ppm) for in-silico spectral library generation.

diann_scan_window.description: Scan window radius for DIA-NN analysis.

diann_scan_window_automatic.description: Let DIA-NN automatically determine the optimal scan window.

diann_mass_acc_automatic.description: Let DIA-NN automatically determine the optimal mass accuracy.

diann_performance_mode.description: Enable DIA-NN high-performance mode (reduces RAM usage at cost of speed).

diann_quick_mass_acc.description: Enable quick mass accuracy estimation in DIA-NN.

diann_random_preanalysis.description: Use a random subset of runs for DIA-NN preliminary analysis.

diann_random_preanalysis_seed.description: Random seed for selecting runs in random preanalysis.

diann_empirical_assembly_ms_n.description: Number of runs used for empirical library assembly in DIA-NN.

diann_skip_preliminary_analysis.description: Skip the preliminary analysis step in DIA-NN.

diann_additional_analysis_args.description: Additional DIA-NN command-line arguments for the main analysis step.
diann_additional_analysis_args.help_text: Any additional DIA-NN flags to pass during the main analysis. Appended to the command after all other arguments.

diann_maxlfq_q.description: Q-value threshold for MaxLFQ quantification in DIA-NN post-processing.

diann_maxlfq_pgq.description: Protein group q-value threshold for MaxLFQ quantification in DIA-NN post-processing.

additional_insilico_args.description: Additional DIA-NN command-line arguments for in-silico library generation.
additional_insilico_args.help_text: Any additional DIA-NN flags to pass during library generation. Appended to the command after all other arguments.

protein_level_fdr_cutoff.description: Protein-level FDR cutoff for DIA-NN quantification.

met_excision.description: Enable N-terminal methionine excision in DIA-NN in-silico digestion.

precursor_mass_tolerance.description: Precursor mass tolerance for database search.

fragment_mass_tolerance.description: Fragment ion mass tolerance for database search.

fragment_mass_tolerance_unit.description: Unit for fragment ion mass tolerance.

species_genes.description: Enable species-specific gene inference in DIA-NN.

diann_export_xic.description: Export extracted ion chromatograms from DIA-NN.

diann_report_decoys.description: Include decoy identifications in DIA-NN report.

contaminant_pattern.description: Pattern to identify contaminant proteins in FASTA database for DIA-NN post-processing.

fragpipe_options.title: FragPipe options
fragpipe_options.description: Parameters for FragPipe-based workflows (DDA LFQ, TMT Label Check, generic FragPipe).

fragpipe_mode.description: FragPipe search mode: pipeline (Nextflow-orchestrated modular execution) or headless (direct FragPipe headless execution).
fragpipe_mode.help_text:
Controls how MSFragger search is executed:
- pipeline: Modular Nextflow-orchestrated execution with per-sample parallelization.
- headless: Run FragPipe headless in a single process, producing results identical to the FragPipe GUI.

fragpipe_workflow.description: Path to a custom FragPipe .workflow file.
fragpipe_workflow.help_text: A FragPipe workflow file (.workflow) containing all tool parameters. If not provided, the pipeline uses a default workflow based on the selected mode and tmt_mode.

fragpipe_container.description: Docker/Singularity image containing licensed FragPipe tools (MSFragger, IonQuant, DiaTracer).
fragpipe_container.help_text: Commercial FragPipe modules require a container with licensed JARs. Provide the full image path (e.g., 'your-registry/fragpipe:24.0'). Open-source modules use the default fcyucn/fragpipe:24.0 image.

tmt_annotation.description: Path to a TMT annotation file mapping channels to sample names.
tmt_annotation.help_text: Required for TMT quantification workflows. Maps TMT channels to sample names.

msfragger_num_slices.description: Number of database slices for split MSFragger search. 1 = no split (default).
msfragger_num_slices.help_text: Controls split-database search for MSFragger. When set to 1 (default), MSFragger searches the full database. When >1, the FASTA database is split into N chunks and searched in parallel. Useful for very large databases where the fragment index exceeds available memory.

decoy_tag.description: Decoy protein tag prefix used by FragPipe/Philosopher.

tmt_type.description: TMT plex type: TMT6, TMT10, TMT11, TMT16, TMT18, TMTPRO.
tmt_type.help_text: Specifies the TMT plex type for TMT workflows. The pipeline auto-selects the appropriate label check workflow file based on this value.

validate_ontologies.description: Validate SDRF ontology terms against the Ontology Lookup Service.

skip_ms_validation.description: Skip mass spectrometry file validation in SDRF check.

skip_factor_validation.description: Skip factor value validation in SDRF check.

skip_experimental_design_validation.description: Skip experimental design validation in SDRF check.

use_ols_cache_only.description: Use only the local OLS cache for ontology validation (no network calls).

file_preparation_options.title: File preparation options
file_preparation_options.description: Parameters controlling raw file conversion and preparation.

local_input_type.description: Expected input file type for local files.

openms_peakpicking.description: Enable OpenMS peak picking before search.

peakpicking_inmemory.description: Use in-memory mode for OpenMS peak picking.

peakpicking_ms_levels.description: MS levels to apply peak picking to (e.g. '1 2').

mzml_features.description: Extract features from mzML files for QC.

decoy_options.title: Decoy database options
decoy_options.description: Parameters for decoy sequence generation (DIA workflows).

add_decoys.description: Add decoy sequences to the protein database.

decoy_string.description: Prefix or suffix string for decoy protein identifiers.

decoy_string_position.description: Position of the decoy string in protein identifiers.

shuffle_max_attempts.description: Maximum attempts for shuffle decoy generation.

shuffle_sequence_identity_threshold.description: Maximum sequence identity allowed between target and decoy.

reporting_options.title: Reporting options
reporting_options.description: Parameters for QC reporting and result export.

pmultiqc_idxml_skip.description: Skip idXML indexing in pmultiqc (faster reporting).

skip_table_plots.description: Skip table plot generation in pmultiqc.

msstats_remove_one_feat_prot.description: Remove proteins with only one feature in MSstats.

ref_condition.description: Reference condition for MSstats contrast comparisons.

contrasts.description: Contrast type for MSstats (pairwise or custom matrix).

msstatslfq_removeFewMeasurements.description: Remove features with few measurements in MSstatsLFQ.

msstatslfq_feature_subset_protein.description: Feature subset method for protein quantification in MSstatsLFQ.

msstatslfq_quant_summary_method.description: Quantification summary method for MSstatsLFQ (TMP or MedianPolish).

reference_proteome_options.title: Reference proteome options
reference_proteome_options.description: UniProt reference proteome options for the workflow.

genome.description: Organism name for reference proteome lookup.

proteomes_base.description: Base URL for UniProt reference proteome FASTA downloads.

proteomes_ignore.description: Do not load the reference proteomes config.

internal_options.title: Internal pipeline options
internal_options.description: Internal parameters. Not intended for direct user configuration.

test_mode.description: Enable test mode with reduced resources (internal).

institutional_config_options.title: Institutional config options
institutional_config_options.description: Parameters used to describe centralised config profiles. These should not be edited.
institutional_config_options.help_text: The centralised nf-core configuration profiles use a handful of pipeline parameters to describe themselves. This information is then printed to the Nextflow log when you run a pipeline. You should not need to change these values when you run a pipeline.

custom_config_version.description: Git commit id for Institutional configs.

custom_config_base.help_text: If you're running offline, Nextflow will not be able to fetch the institutional config files from the internet. If you don't need them, then this is not a problem. If you do need them, you should download the files from the repo and tell Nextflow where to find them with this parameter.

generic_options.title: Generic options
generic_options.description: Less common options for the pipeline, typically set in a config file.
generic_options.help_text:
These options are common to all nf-core pipelines and allow you to customise some of the core preferences for how the pipeline runs.

Typically these options would be set in a Nextflow config file loaded for all pipeline runs, such as `~/.nextflow/config`.

publish_dir_mode.description: Method used to save pipeline results to output directory.
publish_dir_mode.help_text: The Nextflow `publishDir` option specifies which intermediate files should be saved to the output directory. This option tells the pipeline what method should be used to move these files. See [Nextflow docs](https://www.nextflow.io/docs/latest/process.html#publishdir) for details.

email_on_fail.description: Email address for completion summary, only when pipeline fails.
email_on_fail.help_text: An email address to send a summary email to when the pipeline is completed - ONLY sent if the pipeline does not exit successfully.

plaintext_email.description: Send plain-text email instead of HTML.

max_multiqc_email_size.description: File size limit when attaching MultiQC reports to summary emails.

monochrome_logs.description: Do not use coloured log outputs.

hook_url.description: Incoming hook URL for messaging service
hook_url.help_text: Incoming hook URL for messaging service. Currently, MS Teams and Slack are supported.

multiqc_config.description: Custom config file to supply to MultiQC.

multiqc_logo.description: Custom logo file to supply to MultiQC. File name must also be set in the MultiQC config file

multiqc_methods_description.description: Custom MultiQC yaml file containing HTML including a methods description.

validate_params.description: Boolean whether to validate parameters against the schema at runtime

pipelines_testdata_base_path.description: Base URL or local path to location of pipeline test dataset files

trace_report_suffix.description: Suffix to add to the trace report filename. Default is the date and time in the format yyyy-MM-dd_HH-mm-ss.

help_full.description: Display the full detailed help message.

show_hidden.description: Display hidden parameters in the help message (only works when --help or --help_full are provided).

max_cpus.description: Maximum number of CPUs that can be requested for any single job.
max_cpus.help_text: Use to set an upper-limit for the CPU requirement for each process. Should be an integer e.g. --max_cpus 16

max_memory.description: Maximum amount of memory that can be requested for any single job.
max_memory.help_text: Use to set an upper-limit for the memory requirement for each process. Should be a string in the format integer-unit e.g. --max_memory 128.GB

max_time.description: Maximum amount of time that can be requested for any single job.
max_time.help_text: Use to set an upper-limit for the time requirement for each process. Should be a string in the format integer-unit e.g. --max_time 240.h
